item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't disagree with some of the "overly sadistic" comments here. But I think that, in universe, this mostly indicates some researchers who took things too far, more than just, "lol teh Foundation is evulz." Now, when it was written, it may well have been written that way, but with the benefit of being able to look at 5000+ skips in which the Foundation is not nice, but is not evil, that's kind of the way it looks to me by the end of this skip. +1'd because I like the concept though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm torn on this. One one hand, I like the style of writing and the buildup to the test logs. On the other hand, I just think that this seems overly sadistic. It's a similar case to SCP-042, which just feels like downright cruelty rather than serious containment.

Overall I'd give it a weak +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting addition to the article and a unique skip all together. I would also like to see a crosslink with this SCP and SCP-457

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …Ok, i'm sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So are you the original author of this SCP?
If you're not the original author, you can give it down for your own sake, but don't insist on editing someone else's work, please.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SOLVING PUZZLE? MAZE?
WHY DOES THE SCP HAVE TO SOLVE THE PUZZLE?
I know scp foundation is a bastard, and i know they give a pain to scp(like 231. It's a masterpiece).
BUT THEY DIDN'T TORTURE SCP WITH NO REASON!!!
The foundation in 231 is a guys that hardly breaking eggs to make omelet.
IN HERE, THEY'RE JUST FXXKING SADIST!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The audio log made it 100x better. Gives a good insight on the evil side of the Foundation.

item.title: Missing details about interaction.
item.content:encoded: For a humanoid subject, I think the report would benefit from more accurate descriptions of interaction with items and personnel. The subject being of a female form might bring specific ideas to mind, but those are not the focus of my suggestion. Does the "skin" of SCP-054 exhibit any semi-solid resistance? Is it purely liquid and thus penetrable with any object? Is it gelatinous? Solidly glassy if the subject so chooses? It reacted enthusiastically to human interaction before, but was the interaction physical? Can it carry objects? Can it operate equipment without splashing all over it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"The Foundation is cold, not cruel."
Maybe not every individual researcher. Or the higher-ups who approved those tests.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If I'm reading it right (and I might not be), it looks like the researcher that was, essentially, torturing 054 was male. I don't know about you, but if I was being contained and only exposed to one male human on a regular basis, a male human that hurt me for no reason I could discern, I might start to mistrust the entire gender too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can someone explain the "mistrusts males" thing? I've looked through the history and this forum thread and the only clue I have is the thing about the fountain. Anyway, good article.

item.title: Staff Post - Reversion Notice
item.content:encoded:
Revision 29 has been reverted to Revision 28 due to incorrectly changing the object class from Safe to Euclid. While the article does say this:

Subject reclassified Euclid.

At the end of the article it says this:

After five years with no incidents, subject rating has been downgraded to "Safe" on recommendation of Dr. ████████.

Thus confirming that the Object Class is meant to be Safe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well-written, but doesn't grab me enough for an upvote. Not sure why. Still, enjoy the fact that it's at +100 as of this writing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh. I may not have read this since my initial site binge several years ago. Fun article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why did you have to do those experiments? ;;

But, dang it, I like it too much not to upvote. You're horrible.

item.title: Intelligence
item.content:encoded: Usually with most intelligent humanoid SCP's there is IQ level given. I understand if you want to keep the SCP's intelligence a mystery or just use the excuse "How the he'll am I supposed to give an IQ test to something made out of water." Something you should most definitely include however is wether the subject talks and if not how it communicates with the staff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seems odd to use both centigrade and kelvin in the same experiment log, but other than that, seems good.

Effectively torturing what is effectively a cute puppy made from water just to try to understand it's basics principles, well, is probably something the Foundation would do, only stopping when further testing turned out to be potentially very dangerous. That thought makes the skin on my neck feel uncomfortable, and adds a certain extra depth (pun not intended) to this piece. Nice work.

item.title: Re: Downvoted hard.
item.content:encoded: He's allowed to have changed his mind. Bad form to call a user out on their vote unless you're the author requesting further information.

item.title: Re: Downvoted hard.
item.content:encoded:
You realize that you never actually downvoted it, right?

Also, I thought I was sure that sentient beings are never, ever classified Safe.

item.title: Re: Downvoted hard.
item.content:encoded: Yes - it's for those we don't like.

item.title: Downvoted hard.
item.content:encoded: "Maintenance personnel are required to wear non-absorbent NBC suits" - what the hell other kind of NBC suit exists? Is there some kind of porous, absorbent kind that actually soaks up contaminants?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This SCP makes me think of it like a dog which makes it all the more awful. Upvoted!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You horrible horrible people. UV'd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An interesting "bastard douchebag Foundation" piece. UV'd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At first, I was like "Ehhhh… interesting enough". Then I got to the experiment log, and I was like "Oh god you are horrible people. D:" and I couldn't not upvote. ♥

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Any possibility of her separating herself from the mass of water and making herself small enough to, say, hide within a shirt? Because if so, then it would make an interesting log about an attempted escape and force further alienation by making everyone have to wear self contained suits in further interactions

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded: Actually I think you're right, it's distracting and was only there to say "look! weird science!". Changed it to "biology" to be safe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So much better. Upvoted. I still don't like the use of "cryptobiology", but that may just be me being pedantic.

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded: lol, edited. Thanks for the catch. I feel especially stupid 'cuz I'd usually be the first one to nitpick something like that …

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded: Much, much better. Finally, some science is being done around here, with no regard for the consequences. You have my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One note:

Temperature in the enclosure has been lowered to 278 C to help control fifty-four's increasingly erratic behavior.

Did you actually mean that? Or did you mean 278 K (which is closer to 5 C)? Because I doubt you want to keep 054 in a superheated steam phase.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the edits. Certainly seems to nicely reflect the sometimes nasty side of the foundation.

item.title: Re: another edit
item.content:encoded: My last attempt at editing this one. If you see anything you think could be improved, go ahead. If you still don't like it, downvote and it will be cleansed tomorrow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Triage edit to save SCP-054 from the chopping block. The original concept was ok but lacked punch, I thought. When reading it I was wondering what experiments might be done to probe the capabilities of such an entity, and what the effects of those experiments might be on a being that has no understanding of the situation it is in. What do you think?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The date for editing this entry has elapsed. No edit has been attempted. This entry will be deleted Friday (10/2/09).

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded: You know, if you removed the lines about attacking males, and perhaps added a propensity to drowning unprotected personel in general, this might be better…. Fuck I'll get on it I guess

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This entry has been reviewed and slated for editing. If you are the original author, please review this entry and comments, and see what changes could be made. Other users may edit ONLY:

  1. If the original author cannot be found/contacted
  2. If the original author gives permission

If no editing has been done on this entry after a period of two weeks, this entry will come under review for deletion. If you have any questions, please contact a mod or admin.

Start Date: 9/11/09
End Date: 9/25/09

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Awwww, why such a low rating? I personally like it. It's short and sweet. It is not like every SCP has to be excessively long.
